title: [FIRRTL] InferWidths infers width for Analog that's unsatisifiable, should error (?)
body:
The FIRRTL specification says:

> As with integer types, an analog type can represent a multi-bit signal. When analog signals are not given a concrete width, their widths are inferred according to a highly restrictive width inference rule, which requires that the widths of all arguments to a given attach operation be identical.

The following example should be an error (cannot infer a width to make the Analog the same width in all attach's), but instead an inconsistent width is used resulting in an error later in the pipeline:

```
$ <analog-widths.fir 1>&2 | ./build/bin/firtool  --mlir-print-ir-after-failure -format=fir -debug-only=infer-widths --mlir-print-ir-after=firrtl-infer-widths
circuit Widths : 
  module Widths :
    input a : Analog
    output b : Analog<2>
    output c : Analog<1>
    attach(a, b)
    attach(a, c)


===----- Mapping ops to constraint exprs -----===

Expr var1618 for <block argument> of type '!firrtl.analog' at index: 0
Expr 2 for <block argument> of type '!firrtl.analog<2>' at index: 1
Expr 1 for <block argument> of type '!firrtl.analog<1>' at index: 2
Constrained var1618 >= *2
Constrained var1618 >= max(*2, 1)

===----- Constraints -----===

- var1618 >= max(*2, 1)

===----- Checking for unbreakable loops -----===

- Checking var1618 >= max(*2, 1)
      - Visited 2: x >= 2
    - Visited *2: x >= 2
    - Visited 1: x >= 1
  - Visited max(*2, 1): x >= 2
  = Breakable since x >= 2 satisfiable

===----- Solving constraints -----===

- Solving var1618 >= max(*2, 1)
  - Solving max(*2, 1)
    - Solving *2
    = Solved *2 = 2 (unique)
  = Solved max(*2, 1) = 2 (unique)
  - Solved var1618 = 2 (unique)

===----- Update types -----===

Update <block argument> of type '!firrtl.analog' at index: 0 to !firrtl.analog<2>
<stdin>:7:5: error: 'sv.assign' op failed to verify that type should be element of inout type
    attach(a, c)
    ^
<stdin>:7:5: note: see current operation: "sv.assign"(%arg0, %1) : (!hw.inout<i2>, i1) -> ()
// -----// IR Dump After LowerFIRRTLToHW Failed (lower-firrtl-to-hw) //----- //
#loc = loc("<stdin>":3:11)
#loc1 = loc("<stdin>":4:12)
#loc2 = loc("<stdin>":5:12)
"builtin.module"() ({
  "hw.module"() ({
  ^bb0(%arg0: !hw.inout<i2>, %arg1: !hw.inout<i2>, %arg2: !hw.inout<i1>):
    %0 = "sv.read_inout"(%arg1) : (!hw.inout<i2>) -> i2
    %1 = "sv.read_inout"(%arg2) : (!hw.inout<i1>) -> i1
    %2 = "sv.read_inout"(%arg0) : (!hw.inout<i2>) -> i2
    "sv.ifdef"() ({
      "sv.assign"(%arg0, %1) : (!hw.inout<i2>, i1) -> ()
      "sv.assign"(%arg0, %0) : (!hw.inout<i2>, i2) -> ()
      "sv.assign"(%arg2, %2) : (!hw.inout<i1>, i2) -> ()
      "sv.assign"(%arg2, %0) : (!hw.inout<i1>, i2) -> ()
      "sv.assign"(%arg1, %2) : (!hw.inout<i2>, i2) -> ()
      "sv.assign"(%arg1, %1) : (!hw.inout<i2>, i1) -> ()
    }, {
      "sv.ifdef"() ({
        "sv.verbatim"() {format_string = "`error \22Verilator does not support alias and thus cannot arbitrarily connect bidirectional wires and ports\22", symbols = []} : () -> ()
      }, {
        "sv.alias"(%arg0, %arg2, %arg1) : (!hw.inout<i2>, !hw.inout<i1>, !hw.inout<i2>) -> ()
      }) {cond = #sv<macro.ident "verilator">} : () -> ()
    }) {cond = #sv<macro.ident "SYNTHESIS">} : () -> ()
    "hw.output"() : () -> ()
  }) {argLocs = [#loc, #loc1, #loc2], argNames = ["a", "b", "c"], arg_attrs = [{}, {}, {}], comment = "", function_type = (!hw.inout<i2>, !hw.inout<i2>, !hw.inout<i1>) -> (), parameters = [], res_attrs = [], resultLocs = [], resultNames = [], sym_name = "Widths"} : () -> ()
}) : () -> ()
```

I think this is due to this:

https://github.com/llvm/circt/blob/978bccc69d87037e880963c292f186661977525c/lib/Dialect/FIRRTL/Transforms/InferWidths.cpp#L1707-L1711

That is, looks like the constraints from the `Analog<2>` and `Analog<1>` being `>=` the (inferred) width of the `Analog` are not added to the solver.
